name: Warmos
notes: Warmos modulatos Grecian analogos

name: Ambient reverb/tape nodub
notes:
Ripped off from Noynumal on Reddit. Get those dip switches right and hold down microlooper 2 secs until it’s red. 

Mess with the ramping and clock speed to taste.

name: Desert Single Rep Resonator
notes: This is the Single Repeat Resonator with the dipswitch settings of the Desert Ambush (happy accident that sounds cool)

name: Pitch up lofi
notes:
This also sounds good with the pitch to a fifth.

Put it after a delay for extra fun. I’m using an Adineko for good vibes.

name: Relativity
notes: Reverb+stretch at low clock for bitcrushed phrase looping

name: Init with MISO + Trails + Spread
notes: An initialized preset with MISO, Spread, and Trails turned on.

name: Orchestral Synth
notes:
Glue on max.
Alt for Orchestral swell is short feedback almost to fully ccw.
Pitch set to sub octave.
No alt change for impulse synth.

name: Error: Uh Oh
notes:
Additional configuration needed: turn the error blend hidden option full clockwise.

Anything can go wrong in this setting. Each error is equally likely to occur, giving you lots of variation and surprise. Adjust the ERROR knob to have more or less errors overall.

name: Standard
notes: This is what I've been rocking for the meantime.

name: Synth follower
notes: This preset has a synthesizer drifting behind your church-reverberated sound. Works particularly good for slow playing (and chords). It glides towards your primary note in about a second and a half.

name: Freeze and reverse
notes: Use the wet side reverb in latch mode as a freeze, and the left loop side in no-dub mode essentially as a reverse delay. This won't work unless you add some dry loop mix in using the hidden options.

name: Glitch: Swelling Delay
notes: A vaporous pseudo-echo with repeats that gradually swell in and crossfade with one another as you play. These settings are just starters – adjust to taste.

name: Error: Dancing Drone
notes:
Each of the errors are useful in their own way for lending a bit of motion and intrigue to a frozen sound.

Having TYPE set to Playback will introduce momentary pitch shifts that can make a simple freeze feel like a generative composition.

name: Mystical Land
notes: With Onward « Drone » and GL Mystical Land

name: Cloudy
notes: With warped vinyl, loosy  blooper and DD200 tape delay 600/50/50

name: Moving Texture
notes: Play a little, move the clock then enjoyed. Fun with blooper as a delay at the end with modifier turned on

name: Double Shift
notes:
Factory preset Bank 2, left.

A couple of ratcheting pitch shifters, each with their own unique settings. This preset is a fast way to access the combos made possible by R SWAP.

name: Dualing Synth
notes: Hold it a second…

name: Glotchy Geodes
notes: For your rascal purse

name: Slip reverse
notes: Good for some movement between big chords, ideally low in the mix with a big ambient reverb earlier in the chain. Kick in the microlooper on tape mode and play with the modify knob between 10:00 and 2:00 to time travel.

name: As You Were
notes: Tk3 of the album that defined the 90s

name: Ether
notes: Slow Chords with reverb + Gen Loss and Onward + Warped Vinyl

name: Partly Cloudy
notes:
As shared in the Chase Bliss Discord…

Useful Ambience into Tape Echo

Sound sample available on my YouTube channel…

@See-Ess-Dubs

https://youtube.com/shorts/dzNtadpfTZs?feature=share

name: Drone
notes: with lossy and onward « drone »

name: Ether
notes: Slow Chords with reverb + Gen Loss and Onward

name: Cloudy
notes: With lossy, onward blooper and DD200 tape delay 600/50/50

name: Walkthrough “cool trick”
notes: This is a double resonator. Shown in the Chase Bliss walkthrough video of the pedal.

name: Planet Telex
notes: with Gen Loss and reverb and oxford drive 0/25/100 Led/88 + 45/100

name: Swell-catch-looping
notes: Quickly volume swell different notes with the delay engaged, then capture the loop and enjoy messing around with it.

name: Reactive Sustain
notes: I don’t remember what the hidden options are set to, sorry 🙃

name: Q-Tron
notes: Engage hidden option on left toggle to be in the right position while holding both footswitches (envelope set to adaptive)

name: Synth Tower
notes:
Factory preset Bank 2, right.

A resonator and a synthesizer in parallel, both tracking your playing. This preset is a fast way to access the combos made possible by L SWAP.

name: Freeze: Sub synth
notes:
Turn Onward into a doomy synth that follows along and fills out the lower register. As long as OCTAVE is at the minimum position, the rest is up to you.

Experiment with FADE, TEXTURE, and ANIMATE.

name: Frog in my throat Drive
notes: Really sweeping the attack has a lot of fun usable modulation, but this setting has an interesting almost dual voicing that I enjoy.

name: Slightly Singed
notes: Or not so slightly

name: Freeze: Living pad
notes:
Hold the Freeze footswitch down to lock in an infinite pad – the classic freeze effect.

Now experiment with different ERROR settings to introduce motion and variance to bring that pad to life.

name: Vinyl
notes: Vinyl style nice with gen loss failure

name: Light Sleeper
notes:
Factory preset Bank 1, left.

This is the sound of a vintage pitch shifter recorded to tape. An instantly satisfying dose of double nostalgia.

name: Spooky Time
notes: With ->Thermal-> Mood -> Gen Loss Spooky Time Good With Fuzz (at the beginning) or Delay (at the end)

name: Detuned Metallicizer
notes:
Sounds super detuned with open chords or single notes, but does sound very pretty and “metallicy” with bar-chords.

Like a glockenspiel or music box.

name: Slow grains
notes: Good one to play with mix, blend, and glue. Grain settings too. I like playing it with a square wave trem before it using notes sparely.

name: Slow Build
notes: Play around with the dip switches

name: Reverse Drip Alt
notes: With Reverb and slow chord or arpeggio.

name: Shimmering arpeggios
notes: Cane across this sound while noodeling on the guitar, and it was really fun to play with this. The reverb side makes it awesome. Perfect for short tones.

name: Scratch the Needle
notes: Back and forth against the record

name: Follow Gated Decay
notes: Right toggle should show as solid red as latch and no dub are active. Turn on wet side for more reverb

name: Ascend
notes: The notes you play slowly ascend in pitch, going quicker as they climb

name: Lossy-esque
notes: Got some lossy-esque sounds using the phaser and grain tumbler. The goal here is to keep the phaser rate & tumbler feedback as low as possible. Mess with the other settings, especially the poles.

name: Uncle Muscles  Alt.
notes: Find a nice loop then freeze it on the micro looper side. Play with the clock to taste. Than have fun with slip mod on the wet side

name: Sweet arpeggio
notes: Play some arpeggio T+3rd +5 and enjoy

name: Mellowed Fuzz
notes: Highly compressed lofi gluzz

name: House of mirrors
notes: Spread is up enough to create an interesting multi tap and modifier A1 set to 2:30 gets that octave almost shimmering or feeling like you’re bouncing around in a house or mirrors

name: Sweet Berry Wine Alt
notes: Play a Note then launch micro looper than launch the wet channel.

name: Leslie fast
notes: Of course there is no stereo field, but to me this sounds pretty leslie-like

name: raimbow dropz
notes: Now you can see the predator

name: Warp drive
notes: Sounds nice with some crunchy overdrive

name: Drone
notes: With Lossy and Mood « Drone »

name: Freeze: Instant Eno
notes: This setting will turn any sounds that you play into a sprawling ambient composition. Play slowly and be patient; it will take awhile for your new samples to fade in.

name: Mystic
notes: with warped vinyl

name: Distant Nostalgia
notes: Slow-verb into gen lite. Dial in some of your dry signal if you wish. All directions are fun depending on your mood (L>R, L+R, R>L)

name: eVangelis
notes: Pull you out of the chorus

name: Spooky Time
notes: With ->Thermal-> Mood -> Gen Loss Spooky Time Good With Fuzz (at the beginning) or Delay (at the end)

name: J Chippy
notes: Play around with the Micro Loop side and adjust Slip time to taste. Loving this app.. Happy days to you all🤓

name: Pitch the resonator
notes: Clangy chord pads. Really has the compressor chugging and saturated too. Maybe turn your reverb off. Definitely play with both EQs, blend, ramp and mix. All of it really. This is just a starting point!

name: Spectral grains
notes: Not positive about alt settings but the grain alts are worth playing with to dial in taste.

name: Stuttery bright repeats
notes:
Stuttery repeats
Shortish trails
Mess with LENGTH and TIME
BOUNCE enabled but sounds without

Hidden options:
TONE brighter than noon 2 o'clock
STEREO WIDTH max
FADE quite short 10 o'clock 

Tinker as you see fit

name: Ether
notes: Slow Chords with reverb + Gen Loss and Warped Vinyl + Clean

name: Synthy Rhythmic Pulse
notes:
Synthy pulsating rhythmic effect (reminds me of hologram). Use a bpm of 90 to start then experiment. Turn thr texture all way left for bitcrush synth. Enjoy. 

JT.

name: Sweetener
notes: A little warble with some warm static. A cosy sound.

name: Underworld bass synth
notes: Slow songs, maybe mess with time on the right channel to have it catch the notes well, but letting it ring out gets you some fun sounds. Used with a bass, does not bring it down an octave.

name: End Credits
notes:
Factory preset Bank 1, right.

A very big synth run through a very big reverb. It’s a nice gateway to what’s possible on Lost + Found.

name: Desert Ambush
notes: Play slowly and softly, one note at a time

name: Glitch: True Glitch
notes: The middle ERROR TYPE makes it easy to create more natural sounding glitches that pop in and out instead of repeating steadily. Adjust ERROR to control how broken and sporadic the glitches are.

name: Glitch: Auto-repeater
notes:
A classic repeater effect that will sample and infinitely loop each note you play. A simple and fun starting point.

Turn SUSTAIN down and the repeats will cleanly and instantly disappear after a chosen amount of time.

name: Trem/Vibe Boost
notes: Adjust compression for clarity/squish

name: Glitch: Speedy Starter
notes:
Turn up OCTAVE to introduce a secondary, harmonizing voice that plays at 2x speed.

Pairing this with Glitch produces dense phrases with rhythmic interplay that act as great launching points. Play around for a while until you hear a phrase you like, then hold the Glitch footswitch to lock it in.

name: Mystic
notes: with onward + slow chords

name: Arrowroot
notes: Catches the end of a chord beautifully
